current_judgment: a single human-readable retrieval explainer now exists under docs/, and it explains both the repo role split (`src` vs `scripts`) and the current retrieval path from query normalization through graph/rerank to RetrievalResult.
key_evidence: docs/retrieval_flow.md explains the src-layout package model and positions scripts/ as task-oriented operational entrypoints rather than runtime truth owners
the doc points readers to answerer.py, retriever.py, retriever_pipeline.py, retriever_plan.py, retriever_search.py, graph_runtime.py, retriever_rerank.py, and models.py as the main files to open
the retrieval sequence is documented in human terms: normalize -> rewrite/decompose -> BM25 -> vector -> fusion -> graph expand -> rerank -> RetrievalResult -> answer stage

validation_results.section_structure: Select-String -Path docs\retrieval_flow.md -Pattern '^## ' found the expected explainer sections

next_action: if needed, split this into docs/repo_structure.md and docs/answer_flow.md in a follow-up cleanup packet; for now the single retrieval explainer is enough to orient a new reader.
